summary: No secrets are embedded in either manifest, and both connectors expose `catalog` plus `awaiting_connector` for missing primary auth. But there are material executor issues in the Google mail path and Trello credential/path handling that are defensible no-ship risks.

findings.title: `gmail.send` allows RFC822 header injection through unsanitized `to`/`cc`/`subject` fields
findings.recommendation: Reject or sanitize CR/LF in all header-derived inputs before constructing the MIME message. Build structured address/header fields instead of interpolating raw strings into `To:`, `Cc:`, and `Subject:` lines.

findings.title: Missing `TRELLO_BOARD_ID` does not return `awaiting_connector`; it falls through to a remote API error
findings.recommendation: Treat `TRELLO_BOARD_ID` as required connector configuration in `creds()` and raise `NotConnectedError` when absent, so missing connector state consistently returns `{ ok:false, awaiting_connector:true }` per the executor contract.

findings.title: Trello path segments are interpolated without encoding, allowing action args to rewrite the requested API path/query
findings.recommendation: Apply `encodeURIComponent` to `board`, `list_id`, `card_id`, and `to_list_id` before placing them in URL paths, and validate expected ID shape if possible.
